design_philosophy.core_loop: Compete in five distinct skateboarding events (freestyle ramp, high jump, downhill race, downhill jam, pool joust) with escalating difficulty across opponent tiers
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Head-to-head competition against increasingly skilled opponents (Poseur Pete → Aggro Eddie → Bionic Lester) with point-based scoring determining winners
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience the thrill of skateboarding competition without physical risk; perform tricks and downhill races as a progression from amateur to pro skateboarder
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Multi-event sports anthology design inspired by Epyx games (like Summer Games); deliberately structures progression around character-driven rival tiers rather than continuous difficulty scaling. Events are skill-specific rather than narrative-driven, positioning skateboarding as a legitimate competitive sport

difficulty.continues_system: Tournament bracket structure allows sequential play of all five events with up to eight players competing across the full circuit
difficulty.checkpointing: Individual event-based progression; players can challenge events individually or sequentially in tournament mode
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players select which opponent tier to face (beginner, intermediate, advanced) in joust and downhill jam events; this gates access to increasingly challenging rivals

progression.unlock_mechanism: Opponent tiers locked by skill level: Poseur Pete (beginners), Aggro Eddie (intermediate), Bionic Lester (advanced pros); progressing through events sequences a tournament-style competition arc
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer tournament mode supports up to eight players competing sequentially across all five events
Multiple skateboarding disciplines (freestyle, racing, jousting) provide varied gameplay within single game session
Character-based rival progression creates asymmetric challenge curves

world_building_techniques: Character archetypes establish skateboarding subculture: Rodney Recloose (shop owner with Marine Corps tattoo, Rodney Dangerfield resemblance), Bionic Lester (wild youth), Poseur Pete and Aggro Eddie as intermediate rivals
Event settings (skate park, street settings, half-pipes, pools) communicate skateboarding culture spatially
Rival character encounters provide implicit narrative tension within competitive framework

boss_design.philosophy: Opponent tiers function as escalating boss encounters within specific event contexts; each tier (Pete, Eddie, Lester) represents a skill checkpoint rather than traditional boss with attack patterns
notable_bosses: Bionic Lester - advanced-tier joust opponent and downhill jam rival; appears with two cronies in joust event

faction_indicators: Skateboarding skill tiers (beginner/intermediate/pro) as competitive hierarchy
Character archetypes signaling subcultural identity (Lester's green flattop, punk-coded appearance vs. Poseur Pete's implied mainstream posturing)
Rodney's skateshop as community hub for skate culture
danger_signals: Physical joust mechanic (hand-to-hand knockout competition) emphasizing aggressive skill demonstration
Downhill racing in street settings suggesting urban risk-taking
Pool joust as confined combat scenario
environmental_storytelling: Skateshop setting establishes competitive skateboarding as organized community activity
Half-pipe and street environments differentiate freestyle performance from racing contexts
Pool joust location (indoor vs. outdoor park/street races) suggests diverse skateboarding venue culture
